title: What I Want My Grandchildren to Inherit
subtitle: A journal practice for Day 14 of the money arc
excerpt: A set of journal prompts for Day 14. The writing moves from how it feels to be rewriting the family money story, to who in the family deserves a quiet thank-you, to the specific things you want to pass forward.
sourceNotes: Prompt material from Day 14 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: Journal prompts for Day 14, the close of the Week 2 generational arc. Write without editing. The prompts move from the feeling of being a turning point, to the people in your line worth thanking, to the concrete things you are choosing to pass forward.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
How does it feel to know I am rewriting my family's financial story?
Write the feeling plainly, whatever is actually there, including discomfort or doubt.
What legacy am I choosing to pass on?
Name it specifically, a belief, a habit, a way of holding money. What is actually different in the story you are writing?
Closer in
Who in my family, past or present, can I silently thank today?
Name one person. What did they contribute to where you are, even if they didn't know they were doing it?
What do I want my grandchildren to inherit that has nothing to do with money?
The belief, the posture, the relationship with enough. What is the non-financial thing you are passing forward?
What would I need to believe to feel fully settled about being the turning point?
Write the belief that would make the whole thing feel solid. Then write one sentence about whether you are close to believing it yet.
Where this practice comes from
Prompt material from Day 14 of
The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow
(Rebecca J Page, 2025).
